current_hypothesis: Restriction of intramolecular motion (RIM) is the leading working mechanism: aggregation restricts multiple phenyl/torsional motions and suppresses nonradiative decay.
diagnosis: Current leading hypothesis: Restriction of intramolecular motion (RIM) drives AIE by suppressing phenyl/vinyl torsions and other low-frequency motions upon aggregation. Latest evidence leaves this hypothesis unresolved/slightly weakened: the torsion-snapshot vertical excitations show the lowest excited state remains strongly allowed (f ~ 1.02–1.09) with only small energy spread (~0.004 eV) and modest oscillator spread (~0.064) across snapshots, so we do not yet see clear access to a dark/CT state via the sampled torsions. Key remaining uncertainty: whether torsional motion enables nonradiative decay via excited-state relaxation funnels or dark/CT state mixing (not captured by current vertical-only summaries), versus an aggregate-state electronic mechanism (excimer/J) which we cannot check externally due to verifier failure. Best next action: a bounded microscopic “artifact-mining” step to extract transition character/state ordering/CT proxies from the existing .aop/.mo/.stdout outputs for each snapshot, since external verification is currently blocked and this is the highest-yield discriminator still feasible without new heavy computations.
hypothesis_uncertainty_note: RIM remains plausible from rotor-rich structure, but current vertical torsion snapshots show consistently bright S1 and minimal state reordering; thus the core uncertainty is whether nonradiative decay in solution arises from excited-state relaxation along torsions (not captured here) or whether aggregation introduces different emissive electronic states (excimer/J), which cannot be checked due to external verifier unavailability.
capability_assessment: Microscopic tools reliably produce low-cost S0 optimizations and vertical TD manifolds plus limited torsion snapshots, but have limited control over exact dihedrals/angles and do not capture excited-state relaxation/nonradiative funnels. Verifier is currently unavailable (403 region/model), blocking external photophysics/packing discrimination. Macro can only provide qualitative packing/ISC likelihoods from topology/proxies.
stagnation_assessment: No stagnation yet: the main gap has narrowed from general structural plausibility to a specific need for transition-character/state-ordering evidence. Artifact mining is a low-cost, potentially discriminating next step given verifier blockage.
contraction_reason: External evidence retrieval is blocked (verifier 403) and excited-state relaxation is not supported; therefore the plan contracts to extracting maximal discriminating information (state ordering and transition character/CT proxies) from already-generated torsion-snapshot artifacts.

rounds.current_hypothesis: Restriction of intramolecular motion (RIM) is the leading working mechanism: aggregation restricts multiple phenyl/torsional motions and suppresses nonradiative decay.

planner.diagnosis_summary: Current leading hypothesis: Restriction of intramolecular motion (RIM) drives AIE by suppressing phenyl/vinyl torsions and other low-frequency motions upon aggregation. Latest evidence strengthens this hypothesis: macro structure scan sh...
planner.task_instruction: Reuse shared prepared structure (/datasets/workspace/mas_aie/var/runtime/tools/shared_structure/5d41aad8965e/prepared_structure.xyz) and the S0-optimized geometry produced in the prior baseline_bundle run. Low-cost baseline-first: (1) Identify one key aryl–vinyl (stilbene-like) torsion among the 10 torsion candidates (choose a central conjugation-linking bond, not a peripheral phenyl-phenyl bond). (2) Generate exactly two torsion-perturbed geometries by twisting that dihedral by approximately +25° and -25° relative to the S0-optimized value (no re-optimization). (3) Run torsion_snapshot_follow_up vertical excitations (S1–S5) at the same low-cost level used previously, on the two perturbed geometries. Report for each: excitation energies and oscillator strengths; highlight changes to the first bright state (energy shift and Δf). Keep within the bounded snapshot scope; do not do scans or TS searches; do not decide the global mechanism.

agent_task_instructions.microscopic: Reuse shared prepared structure (/datasets/workspace/mas_aie/var/runtime/tools/shared_structure/5d41aad8965e/prepared_structure.xyz) and the S0-optimized geometry from the prior run. Low-cost baseline-first: pick one central aryl–vinyl torsion; create two single-point twisted geometries (~+25° and ~-25° from the S0 value; no re-opt). Run torsion_snapshot_follow_up vertical excitations (S1–S5) on both. Report excitation energies + oscillator strengths and summarize the change in the first bright state (ΔE, Δf). No scans, no TS, no global mechanism choice.

planner.hypothesis_uncertainty_note: RIM is supported by high rotor count/branching and an intrinsically bright lowest excited state, but remains uncertain because we have not yet shown that modest torsional distortion meaningfully deactivates (reduced f or state reordering) or that aggregate-specific electronic states (excimer/J) are absent; current evidence is single-molecule vertical excitation only.
planner.capability_assessment: Macro: reliable for topology/rotor/planarity proxies but cannot predict packing/excimer energetics. Microscopic: baseline_bundle succeeded (aTB S0 opt + TD vertical S1–S5) but the requested torsion sensitivity was not executed in the prior run (partial_observable_only); however, the agent indicates torsion_snapshot_follow_up is within supported scope and should be attempted explicitly next.
planner.stagnation_assessment: No stagnation: this is the first refinement round with new macro + microscopic evidence; the next step targets the largest unresolved discriminator (torsion sensitivity) within current computational capability.
planner.contraction_reason: Microscopic prior run returned only baseline_bundle outputs (no torsion snapshots) due to partial_observable_only; macro could not provide packing/excimer/ISC quantification due to capability limits.

working_memory.evidence_summary:
macro: Task completion: Task was completed only in a capability-limited contracted form. The agent returned bounded macro evidence, but it could not complete unsupported parts of the Planner instruction: Any explicit aggregate-state packing prediction, crystal/stacking model, or excimer binding-energy computation; Any quantitative ISC rate estimation or excited-state electronic-structure calculation; Any global mechanism adjudication or final selection among hypotheses.
Task understanding: Interpret the Planner instruction as a macro-level structural analysis task for the current working hypothesis "Restriction of intramolecular motion (RIM) is the leading working mechanism: aggregation restricts multiple phenyl/torsional motions and suppresses nonradiative decay.": Using the provided canonical SMILES and shared structure context (rotatable bonds=10, aromatic rings=10, planarity/compactness proxies, heteroatoms N2S in a fused nsnc ring), produce a qualitative AIE mechanism screening note. (1) Identify dominant intramolecular motion degrees (phenyl rotations, vinyl twists) likely to enable nonradiative decay in solution; (2) assess likelihood of aggregate-state electronic emission (excimer/J-aggregate) given size/bulk/shape; (3) comment on whether heteroaromatic nsnc-thio ring could introduce ISC/heavy-atom effects that would compete with fluorescence; (4) list 2–3 discriminating experimental signatures one would expect for RIM vs aggregate-excimer vs conformational-dark-state mechanisms. Do not choose the global final mechanism; just provide structured pros/cons and discriminators.
Shared 3D structure context is available and reused from /datasets/workspace/mas_aie/var/runtime/tools/shared_structure/5d41aad8965e/prepared_structure.xyz.
This agent should only summarize low-cost structural evidence and should not make a global mechanism judgment or recommend a system-level next action.
Reasoning summary: From topology and provided geometry proxies: the molecule is very large (101 atoms) and highly aromatic (10 rings) with many branch points and 10 rotatable bonds, implying multiple low-frequency torsions. Key motion DOFs are likely (i) aryl–aryl dihedral rotations at biaryl linkages and (ii) twisting/relaxation around exocyclic C=C (stilbene-like) linkages connecting phenyls, which can open nonradiative pathways (TICT-like or twisted excited-state funnels) in solution. However, the shared planarity/compactness proxies are high (~0.97/0.95), suggesting an overall fairly extended-conjugated, shape-persistent conformer in the selected structure; thus emission quenching in solution would more likely come from local torsions (peripheral phenyl rotors / vinyl twists) than from wholesale backbone folding. Aggregate-state excimer/J-aggregate emission is plausible because of the large planar aromatic surface area (π–π propensity), but significant peripheral phenyl crowding/branching can frustrate close cofacial stacking; this makes classic excimer formation less certain and shifts expectation toward RIM-dominated brightening upon aggregation (restricted phenyl/vinyl motions) unless specific packing enables slipped stacks. The N2S fused heteroaromatic ring can increase spin–orbit coupling relative to all-carbon analogs (S contributes more than C/N), potentially enhancing ISC and triplet yield; whether it strongly competes with fluorescence depends on how electronically involved that ring is in the emissive excited state. Qualitative discriminators: RIM typically shows viscosity/temperature dependence and minimal new red-shifted bands; excimer/J shows concentration/aggregate-dependent new broad/red-shifted emission with altered lifetimes; conformational dark-state mechanisms show strong solvatochromism or dual emission and clear signatures of twisted charge-transfer/dark conformers.
Capability limit note: This note can only use deterministic structural/topology cues and the provided geometry proxies; it cannot simulate aggregation/packing, compute excited-state surfaces, or quantify ISC/excimer energetics. Conclusions must be framed as qualitative likelihoods with proposed discriminating experiments.
Execution plan: Use deterministic_macro_structure_scan to execute the bounded macro workflow with focus areas: Topology-based rotor inventory from canonical SMILES (biaryl linkages, stilbene-like C=C connections), Use shared proxies: rotatable bonds=10, aromatic rings=10, planarity_proxy~0.97, compactness_proxy~0.95, branch_point_count=18, principal_span_proxy~156, Qualitative packing propensity inference only (no simulation): planar π-surface vs steric hindrance from phenyl crowding, Qualitative ISC risk reasoning from presence of S (and N) in fused heteroaromatic ring. Planned local steps: [shared_context_load] Load the shared prepared structure context and reuse its descriptors. [focus_selection] Select the macro structural focus areas requested by the Planner instruction. [topology_analysis] Summarize rotor topology, ring systems, conjugation, and donor-acceptor layout. [geometry_proxy_analysis] Summarize planarity, torsion, compactness, and conformer-dispersion proxies.

Reasoning summary: Local reasoning summary: Given the task constraints and the availability of a shared prepared structure, the baseline_bundle route is the most suitable. It supports low-cost aTB S0 optimization and vertical excited-state manifold characterization, which aligns with the requested deliverables. The torsion sensitivity step can be incorporated as a bounded snapshot follow-up within this route by generating a small number of single-point calculations with modest torsion perturbations. This approach respects the budget profile and avoids unsupported heavy computations. The shared prepared structure will be reused to minimize computational overhead.
Capability limit note: The baseline_bundle route supports low-cost aTB S0 optimization and vertical excitation calculations but does not support full torsion scans or transition-state optimizations. The torsion sensitivity analysis is limited to at most two single-point vertical excitation calculations on modestly twisted geometries, consistent with the bounded approach.
Failure policy: If the aTB S0 optimization fails, report the failure with error details and do not proceed to vertical excitation calculations. If vertical excitation calculations fail, report the failure and provide any partial data available. If torsion-perturbed single-point calculations fail, report the failure and provide results from the unperturbed geometry only.

[s1_vertical_excitation] Run a bounded real Amesp vertical excitation calculation at the best available low-cost geometry to characterize the low-lying excited-state manifold without escalating to heavy excited-state optimization.

working_memory.information_gain_assessment: Moderate-to-high expected gain: a two-point torsion snapshot directly tests whether twisting along a representative coordinate substantially reduces oscillator strength or changes state ordering, which is a key discriminator between RIM/motion-enabled quenching vs mechanisms dominated by aggregate-specific electronic states.
working_memory.gap_trend: Narrowing: moved from purely structural plausibility to electronic evidence that S1 is intrinsically bright; remaining gap is now specifically torsion-sensitivity/relaxation rather than general emissivity.

planner.task_instruction: Retrieve external experimental/photophysical evidence for molecule C58H40N2S (InChIKey: IRRPIWGPVSMOGZ-UHFFFAOYSA-N). Specifically extract: (1) whether it is reported as AIE/AIEE and the solvent/aggregation conditions; (2) emission spectra in solution vs aggregate/solid (look for new broad red-shifted bands suggesting excimer/exciplex/J-aggregate); (3) fluorescence lifetimes/quantum yields changes upon aggregation; (4) any viscosity/temperature dependence data; (5) any crystal/packing description (π–π stacking distances, slipped stacks) if available. Return concise citations and key discriminating observations only.

agent_task_instructions.verifier: Retrieve external experimental/photophysical evidence for molecule C58H40N2S (InChIKey: IRRPIWGPVSMOGZ-UHFFFAOYSA-N). Specifically extract: (1) whether it is reported as AIE/AIEE and the solvent/aggregation conditions; (2) emission spectra in solution vs aggregate/solid (look for new broad red-shifted bands suggesting excimer/exciplex/J-aggregate); (3) fluorescence lifetimes/quantum yields changes upon aggregation; (4) any viscosity/temperature dependence data; (5) any crystal/packing description (π–π stacking distances, slipped stacks) if available. Return concise citations and key discriminating observations only.

planner.hypothesis_uncertainty_note: RIM remains plausible from high rotor count/branching and AIE-like structural motifs, but the low-cost torsion snapshots did not show large suppression of oscillator strength or state reordering; thus, the critical missing piece is evidence for excited-state relaxation/nonradiative pathways (not captured here) or external experimental signs pointing instead to aggregate-state electronic emission (excimer/J) versus motion-restriction.
planner.capability_assessment: Macro is limited to topology/proxy inference and cannot determine packing/excimer energetics or ISC rates. Microscopic Amesp runs succeeded for baseline_bundle and torsion_snapshot_follow_up, but the torsion follow-up did not enforce the exact ±25° central aryl–vinyl dihedral perturbation; instead it generated up to 6 snapshots on two dihedrals with fixed targets (±120°, 0°), and still only provides vertical excitation/oscillator metrics (no excited-state relaxation or nonradiative-rate estimates).
planner.stagnation_assessment: No stagnation yet: we obtained new torsion-snapshot electronic evidence, but it does not strongly discriminate RIM versus alternative pathways; moving to external verification is now the highest-yield step.
planner.contraction_reason: Microscopic torsion follow-up could not be executed exactly as specified (two ±25° snapshots on a chosen central aryl–vinyl bond); the available route generated a bounded set of 6 torsion snapshots on detected dihedrals with preset angles, and provided only vertical excitation summaries.

Task understanding: Interpret the Planner instruction as a bounded low-cost microscopic Amesp task for the current working hypothesis "Restriction of intramolecular motion (RIM) is the leading working mechanism: aggregation restricts multiple phenyl/torsional motions and suppresses nonradiative decay.": Reuse shared prepared structure (/datasets/workspace/mas_aie/var/runtime/tools/shared_structure/5d41aad8965e/prepared_structure.xyz) and the S0-optimized geometry from the prior run. Low-cost baseline-first: pick one central aryl–vinyl torsion; create two single-point twisted geometries (~+25° and ~-25° from the S0 value; no re-opt). Run torsion_snapshot_follow_up vertical excitations (S1–S5) on both. Report excitation energies + oscillator strengths and summarize the change in the first bright state (ΔE, Δf). No scans, no TS, no global mechanism choice.
Requested local focus: low-cost aTB S0 geometry optimization, vertical excited-state manifold characterization, torsion-sensitivity summary
Requested Amesp route summary: Perform torsion snapshot follow-up by creating two single-point twisted geometries (~+25° and ~-25° torsion angles) from the S0 optimized geometry and compute vertical excitations (S1–S5) on both without re-optimization.
Recent round context: Recent round 1 used action 'macro, microscopic' and left the gap 'Direct discriminating evidence that key torsional motions modulate brightness/state character (torsion-snapshot sensitivity) and thus can plausibly control nonradiative decay; and secondarily, lack of aggregate-state electronic-state evidence (excimer/J) which is outside current deterministic tools without external data.'.

Reasoning summary: Local reasoning summary: Given the task constraints and the available computational capabilities, the torsion_snapshot_follow_up route is the most appropriate. It allows bounded torsion sensitivity analysis by generating a small number of snapshot geometries and performing vertical excitation calculations without geometry re-optimization. This approach aligns with the low-cost balanced budget profile and avoids unsupported tasks such as scans or TS optimizations. The shared prepared structure and prior S0-optimized geometry will be reused to minimize computational cost and ensure consistency.
Capability limit note: The current microscopic capabilities support only bounded torsion snapshot follow-ups with a small number of snapshots and vertical excitation calculations. Full torsion scans, transition state optimizations, and excited-state relaxations are not supported at this time.
Failure policy: If the torsion snapshot generation or vertical excitation calculations fail, report the failure locally with error details and do not attempt unsupported fallback routes. Ensure that partial results, if any, are saved for further analysis.
Execution plan: Execute the real bounded Amesp route "torsion_snapshot_follow_up" as follows: [torsion_snapshot_generation] Generate bounded torsion snapshots for at most 6 snapshot geometries, without running a full scan or TS/IRC. [s0_optimization] Run a real low-cost Amesp aTB S0 geometry optimization on the prepared 3D structure. [s1_vertical_excitation] Run a bounded real Amesp vertical excitation calculation at the best available low-cost geometry to characterize the low-lying excited-state manifold without escalating to heavy excited-state optimization.
Expected outputs from this bounded run: snapshot geometry labels, snapshot vertical-state proxies, torsion sensitivity summary
If Amesp fails, return the available partial artifacts and local uncertainty only.
Result summary: Amesp route 'torsion_snapshot_follow_up' finished with final_energy_hartree=-123.2527088517, homo_lumo_gap_ev=1.6241771, rmsd_from_prepared_structure_angstrom=1.445818, and 101 Mulliken charges. Bounded S1 vertical excitation returned first_excitation_energy_ev=3.0602 and first_oscillator_strength=1.0271 across 5 states. First bright state energy=3.0602 with pattern=lowest_state_is_bright. Route summary={'snapshot_count': 6, 'torsion_sensitivity_summary': {'excitation_spread_ev': 0.0039, 'oscillator_spread': 0.0637}, 'torsion_sensitive': True}.
Remaining local uncertainty: Microscopic local uncertainty after this Amesp run: this bounded Amesp route 'torsion_snapshot_follow_up' does not adjudicate the global mechanism. it does not execute full-DFT or heavy excited-state optimization. it also leaves unsupported local requests unresolved: torsion scan; transition-state optimization. targeted follow-up remains bounded by current Amesp route availability and resource limits.

working_memory.information_gain_assessment: High expected gain from verifier: literature spectra/lifetimes/packing can directly confirm AIE behavior and distinguish RIM (viscosity dependence, minimal new bands) from excimer/J-aggregate signatures (new broad/red-shifted bands, different lifetimes, defined π–π stacks).
working_memory.gap_trend: Partially narrowing: we tested vertical torsion sensitivity and found only modest changes, shifting the main discriminator toward experimental photophysics/packing evidence rather than further low-cost vertical calculations.

rounds.current_hypothesis: Restriction of intramolecular motion (RIM) is the leading working mechanism: aggregation restricts multiple phenyl/torsional motions and suppresses nonradiative decay.

planner.diagnosis_summary: External supervision (verifier) produced no evidence cards due to a hard retrieval failure (403 region/model unavailable), so it provides neither support nor conflict for the current leading RIM hypothesis. Internal evidence still modest...
planner.task_instruction: Using the completed round_02 torsion_snapshot_follow_up artifacts (the 6 snapshot geometries and their S1–S5 vertical excitation outputs), extract for each snapshot: (1) dominant orbital transitions for S1 and the first bright state (indices, e.g., HOMO->LUMO) if available from the .mo/.aop outputs; (2) a qualitative CT proxy (e.g., spatial separation/fragment localization of the involved MOs, or any provided CT metric); (3) whether any snapshot shows a low-oscillator-strength lowest excited state below a bright state (state reordering/dark S1). Summarize in a compact table and report whether a torsion-accessible dark/CT state is suggested by the snapshot manifold. Keep strictly within existing outputs; do not run new scans, re-optimizations, or excited-state relaxations; do not decide the global mechanism.

agent_task_instructions.microscopic: Using the completed round_02 torsion_snapshot_follow_up artifacts (6 snapshots + their S1–S5 outputs), extract per snapshot: dominant orbital transitions for S1 and first bright state; qualitative CT/localization proxy from MO data if available; check for state reordering (dark lowest state below bright). Provide a compact table + brief interpretation. No new calculations, no scans, no excited-state optimization, no global mechanism decision.

planner.hypothesis_uncertainty_note: RIM remains the best-supported among current hypotheses from rotor-rich structure and bright vertical S1, but is now only moderately supported because vertical torsion snapshots did not strongly change oscillator strength; decisive evidence would require either (i) experimental photophysics/packing (currently blocked by verifier failure) or (ii) computations capturing excited-state relaxation/nonradiative funnels, which are outside current bounded routes.
planner.capability_assessment: Verifier: hard failure (403 region/model) prevented any external evidence collection. Microscopic: can compute low-cost vertical manifolds and bounded torsion snapshots, but has limited control over exact dihedrals/angles and cannot do excited-state relaxation or nonradiative-rate estimation; however, it may still mine existing .mo/.aop artifacts for transition character/CT proxies and state-ordering diagnostics. Macro: remains qualitative only for packing/ISC likelihoods.
planner.stagnation_assessment: No stagnation yet: prior rounds generated new internal electronic-structure evidence; the next step is a bounded, high-specificity re-analysis of existing microscopic outputs to discriminate dark/CT-state accessibility without additional heavy computation.
planner.contraction_reason: External verifier path is unavailable (hard 403), so the plan contracts to extracting additional discriminating information from already-produced torsion snapshot electronic-structure artifacts rather than attempting new external retrieval or heavier computations.
